back_translation: Of David. Do not fret over those who do evil; do not envy those who do wrong.
For soon they will wither like grass and fade like green plants.
Trust in the LORD and do good; dwell in the land and cultivate faithfulness.
Delight yourself in the LORD, and He will give you the desires of your heart.
Commit your way to the LORD; trust in Him, and He will act.
He will make your righteousness shine like the dawn, and your justice like the noonday sun.
Be still before the LORD and wait patiently for Him; do not fret when the wicked prosper in their ways, when they carry out wicked schemes.
Refrain from anger and forsake wrath; do not fret—it only brings harm.
For those who do evil will be cut off, but those who hope in the LORD will inherit the land.
Yet a little while, and the wicked will be no more; though you look for their place, it will not be found.
But the humble will inherit the land and delight in abundant prosperity.
The wicked plot against the righteous and gnash their teeth at them,
but the Lord laughs at them, for He sees that their day is coming.
The wicked draw the sword and bend the bow to bring down the poor and needy, and to slay those who walk uprightly.
But their swords will pierce their own hearts, and their bows will be broken.
The little that the righteous has is better than the abundance of many wicked.
For the arms of the wicked will be broken, but the LORD upholds the righteous.
The LORD knows the days of the blameless, and their inheritance will endure forever.
In the time of trouble they will not be ashamed, and in the days of famine they will be satisfied.
But the wicked and the enemies of the LORD will perish; they will vanish like the glory of the pastures, fading away like smoke.
The wicked borrow and do not repay, but the righteous are merciful and giving.
Surely those whom He blesses will inherit the land, but those whom He curses will be cut off.
The LORD orders the steps of a man and delights in his way.
Though he falls, he will not be left prostrate, for the LORD holds his hand.
I was once a child and now am old, yet I have never seen the righteous forsaken, nor their children begging for bread.
He is ever merciful and lends, and his children are a blessing.
Turn away from evil and do good, and you will abide forever.
For the LORD loves justice and will not forsake His faithful ones; they are preserved forever, but the offspring of the wicked will be cut off.
The righteous will inherit the land and dwell in it forever.
The mouth of the righteous utters wisdom, and his tongue speaks justice.
The law of his God is in his heart; his steps do not falter.
The wicked lie in wait for the righteous and seek to kill him,
but the LORD will not leave him in the power of the wicked, nor let him be condemned when he is judged.
Wait for the LORD and keep His way, and He will exalt you to inherit the land; when the wicked are cut off, you will see it.
I have seen a wicked, ruthless man flourishing like a green tree in its native soil,
but soon he passed away and was no more; though I searched, I could not find him.
Consider the blameless and observe the upright, for the future of the man of peace is prosperous.
But the transgressors will all be destroyed; the future of the wicked will be cut off.
The salvation of the righteous comes from the LORD; He is their stronghold in time of trouble.
The LORD helps them and delivers them; He rescues them and saves them from the wicked, because they take refuge in Him.
